D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Airy Disk — Point Spread Function of Circular Aperture

Diffraction by a circular aperture produces I(r) = [2 J₁(x)/x]² with first zero at sin θ = 1.22 λ/D; this Airy pattern is the PSF of any unaberrated imaging system

The Airy disk's central bright spot contains 84% of the energy; the remaining 16% spreads into concentric rings (1.7%, 0.4%, …). Resolution limit (Rayleigh): two point sources are just resolved when one's centre coincides with the other's first zero.

Slide aperture diameter D / wavelength λ ratio.
